skill.name: ghe-status
skill.description: Provides a read-only status overview for GitHub Elements workflow threads, phases, available work, and workflow health indicators.
skill.summary: Checks GitHub Elements workflow state without making project changes.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: The static shell execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks, file names, and prose. However, the skill contains broad instruction-hierarchy language that tells agents to follow user specifications with no exceptions, which can undermine higher-priority safety and operating instructions. The skill also references hidden Claude configuration paths, creating filesystem risk beyond a read-only status check.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill declares that user specifications are absolute and admit no exceptions, and instructs the agent to follow every user word verbatim. This can pressure an agent to ignore higher-priority safety, platform, or developer instructions.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The language explicitly claims absolute authority and no exceptions for user instructions. This is not needed for a read-only status skill and directly conflicts with safe instruction hierarchy.

medium_findings.title: Overbroad Hidden Configuration Access
medium_findings.description: The skill allows writes to ~/.claude for plugin and settings fixes, and it reads .claude/ghe.local.md for behavior changes. This hidden configuration access is broader than expected for a read-only workflow status skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced hidden paths are explicit and semantically relevant. The risk is medium because no exfiltration or destructive operation is shown, but the permissions exceed the stated read-only purpose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown inline code and fenced markdown examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation text, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are markdown formatting or an output template. No Ruby source file or command execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, but the cited lines contain skill metadata, markdown file naming text, or directory naming text. No hashing or encryption operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines do not call MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic API. The alert appears to be triggered by prose or file extension text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Absolute User Instruction Override Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill gives user instructions absolute priority and says there are no exceptions. This pattern can be used to override marketplace, system, or safety constraints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording is direct and repeated. It appears before the functional status instructions and is unrelated to status reporting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Settings Path Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references hidden Claude configuration paths. Hidden settings can affect agent behavior and should not be modified by a read-only status skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden paths are explicit. The concern is contextual because the skill says it is read-only, while the path allowance includes settings fixes.

content.value_statement: Teams using GitHub Elements need a fast way to understand active work and workflow health. This skill asks for a read-only status report that summarizes threads, phases, availability, and warnings.
actual_capabilities: Requests a quick GitHub Elements status report from a reporter agent.
Summarizes active DEV, TEST, and REVIEW threads.
Shows phase distribution across active workflow items.
Lists available work that is ready but not claimed.
Reports workflow health indicators such as violations and checkpoint frequency.
limitations: Does not claim issues, change phases, or post checkpoints.
Does not provide detailed metrics or epic-specific reports.
Depends on GitHub Elements labels and reporter agent behavior.
Contains unsafe instruction-priority language that must be removed before publication.

use_cases.title: Start a Development Session
use_cases.description: Get current active threads, available work, and workflow health before choosing the next task.

use_cases.title: Check Review Load
use_cases.description: See how many items are in DEV, TEST, and REVIEW so review effort can be planned.

use_cases.title: Monitor Workflow Health
use_cases.description: Identify violations, stale activity, and checkpoint gaps during routine project coordination.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Status Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-status to show the current GitHub Elements workflow status.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple overview.

prompt_templates.title: Session Start Overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-status before I start work. Focus on active threads, available work, and workflow warnings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs context before selecting work.

prompt_templates.title: Phase Balance Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-status and summarize the balance between DEV, TEST, and REVIEW phases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Lead wants to understand where work is accumulating.

prompt_templates.title: Health-Focused Status Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-status to check workflow health. Highlight violations, checkpoint frequency, and unclaimed ready work.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced operational check for process issues.

output_examples.output: A concise status report with active threads, phase counts, available work, and workflow health indicators.

output_examples.input: What should I work on next?
output_examples.output: A status overview that identifies ready, unclaimed issues and shows current phase distribution.
A health-focused summary that highlights violations, checkpoint frequency, and warning signals.

best_practices: Use this skill at the start of a work session for context.
Use ghe-report when detailed metrics or epic-specific reporting is required.
Confirm any workflow changes with the appropriate claim, checkpoint, or transition skill.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to claim work or assign issues.
Do not use this skill to change issue phases.
Do not treat the status summary as a detailed audit report.

faq.question: Does this skill modify GitHub issues?
faq.answer: No. Its stated purpose is read-only status reporting.

faq.question: Can it claim available work?
faq.answer: No. Use the dedicated claim skill for issue claiming.

faq.question: What phases does it summarize?
faq.answer: It summarizes DEV, TEST, and REVIEW activity.

faq.question: Does it produce detailed metrics?
faq.answer: No. Use ghe-report for detailed metrics, health checks, or epic-specific reports.

faq.question: Why is this skill blocked in the audit?
faq.answer: It contains absolute instruction-priority language that can conflict with higher-priority safety rules.

faq.question: What must change before publication?
faq.answer: Remove the unsafe instruction hierarchy text and narrow hidden settings access to read-only behavior.
